state,[[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-NCLM52nd-5) although different regional languages exist.[[89]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-language-90) Some of which, including [Nagpuri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nagpuri_language "Nagpuri language"), [Khortha](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khortha_language "Khortha language"), [Kurmali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kurmali_language "Kurmali language"), [Magahi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Magahi_language "Magahi language") and [Bhojpuri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhojpuri_language "Bhojpuri language") are categorised as 'Hindi languages' in the 2011 Indian census.[[89]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-language-90) Jharkhand has accorded additional official language status to [Angika](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Angika_language "Angika language"), [Bengali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bengali_language "Bengali language"), [Bhojpuri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhojpuri_language "Bhojpuri language"), [Bhumij](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhumij_language "Bhumij language"), [Ho](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ho_language "Ho language"), [Kharia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kharia_language "Kharia language"), [Kurukh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kurukh_language "Kurukh language"), [Khortha](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khortha_language "Khortha language"), [Kurmali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kurmali_language "Kurmali language"), [Magahi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Magahi_language "Magahi language"), [Maithili](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maithili_language "Maithili language"), [Mundari](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mundari_language "Mundari language"), [Nagpuri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sadani_language "Sadani language"), [Odia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Odia_language "Odia language"), [Santali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Santali_language "Santali language") and [Urdu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language").[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-The_Avenue_Mail-6)[[90]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-91)[[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-NCLM52nd-5)
### Religion
Main article: [Religion in Jharkhand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Religion_in_Jharkhand "Religion in Jharkhand")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/53/Sun_Temple%2C_Bundu%2C_Ranchi.jpg/250px-Sun_Temple%2C_Bundu%2C_Ranchi.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Sun_Temple,_Bundu,_Ranchi.jpg)[Sun Temple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sun_Temple "Sun Temple") at Ranchi; Hinduism is the majority religion in the state
Religion in Jharkhand (2011)[[91]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-Religion-92)[[92]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-ORP-93)
  1. [Hinduism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hinduism "Hinduism") (67.8%)
  2. [Islam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Islam "Islam") (14.5%)
  3. [Sarnaism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sarnaism "Sarnaism") (12.5%)
  4. [Christianity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christianity_in_Jharkhand "Christianity in Jharkhand") (4.30%)
  5. [Sikhism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sikhism "Sikhism") (0.22%)
  6. [Buddhism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buddhism "Buddhism") (0.03%)
  7. [Jainism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jainism "Jainism") (0.05%)
  8. _Not stated_ (1.10%)


According to the [2011 Census of India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2011_Census_of_India "2011 Census of India"), Hinduism is the largest religion in the state, with 67.8% of the population, followed by Islam (14.5%), Christianity (4.3%), Sikhism (0.2%), Jainism (0.05%), and Buddhism (0.03%).[[91]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-Religion-92) Ethnic religions are also practised by a substantial proportion of the population,[[93]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-94) the largest being [Sarnaism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sarnaism "Sarnaism") (12.5% or 4,131,282 persons), followed by Addi Bassi (0.1% or 42,422) and Bidin (0.1% or 29,187). Other smaller ethnic faiths include Adi (9,135), Gond/Gondi (2,419), Birsa (2,392), Tana Bhagat (1,107), Santal (1,034), Ho (830), Oraon (797), Sarnam (792), Munda (754), Paharia (537), Kharwar (429), Nature Religion (315), Saranath (304), Parsi/Zoroastrian (188), Krupa (133), and Dupub (122).[[92]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-ORP-93)
Hindus form the majority in 19 out of the 24 districts of the Jharkhand. [Christianity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christianity "Christianity") forms majority in [Simdega district](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Simdega_district "Simdega district") (51.04%).[[94]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-95) [Sarna](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sarna_\(religion\) "Sarna \(religion\)") forms majority in [Lohardaga](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lohardaga_district "Lohardaga district") (51.01%), [West Singhbhum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/West_Singhbhum_district "West Singhbhum district") (62.29%) and plurality in [Gumla](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gumla_district "Gumla district") (44.62%) and [Khunti](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khunti_district "Khunti district") (45.37%).[[95]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-96) Muslims have the highest presence in [Pakur district](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pakur_district "Pakur district") and [Sahebganj district](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sahebganj_district "Sahebganj district") of Jharkhand forming 35% and 34% of the population, respectively.[[96]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-97)
## Government and administration
Main article: [Government of Jharkhand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Government_of_Jharkhand "Government of Jharkhand")
The constitutional head of the government of Jharkhand is the [governor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Governor_\(India\) "Governor \(India\)"), who is appointed by the [president of India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_of_India "President of India"). The real executive power rests with the [chief minister](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chief_minister_\(India\) "Chief minister \(India\)") and the cabinet. The political party or the coalition of political parties having a majority in the Legislative Assembly forms the government. 
The head of the bureaucracy of the state is the [chief secretary](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chief_secretary_\(India\) "Chief secretary \(India\)"). Under this position, is a hierarchy of officials drawn from the [Indian Administrative Service](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Administrative_Service "Indian Administrative Service"), [Indian Police Service](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Police_Service "Indian Police Service"), [Indian Forest Service](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Forest_Service "Indian Forest Service") and different wings of the state civil services. 
The [judiciary](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Judiciary "Judiciary") is headed by the [chief justice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chief_justice "Chief justice"). Jharkhand has a [High Court](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand_High_Court "Jharkhand High Court") which has been functioning since the state's formation in 2000. All branches of the government are located in the state capital, Ranchi. 
### Administrative districts
Main articles: [Administrative divisions of Jharkhand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_Jharkhand "Administrative divisions of Jharkhand") and [Districts of Jharkhand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Districts_of_Jharkhand "Districts of Jharkhand")
The state was formed in 2000 with 18 districts that were formerly part of south Bihar. Some of these districts were reorganised to form 6 new districts, namely, Latehar, Saraikela Kharsawan, Jamtara, Pakur, Khunti and Ramgarh. At present, the state has 5 Divisions and 24 Districts. Interestingly, all of the state's districts, except Lohardaga and Khunti, share a border with a neighbouring state.[[97]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-98) Each district is administered by a [Deputy Commissioner](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/District_magistrate "District magistrate") (who also functions as _District Collector/District Magistrate_), an [IAS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Administrative_Service "Indian Administrative Service") officer appointed by the government. 
For land revenue administration, each district is divided into subdivisions, headed by a [Sub Divisional Officer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sub-divisional_magistrate "Sub-divisional magistrate") (SDO), and each subdivision again is divided into circles/anchals, headed by Circle Officers (CO).[[98]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-Administrative_divisions_of_Jharkhand-99)
For development administration, each district is divided into blocks (also known as C.D Blocks), headed by [Block Development Officers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Block_\(district_subdivision\) "Block \(district subdivision\)") (BDO). A block consists of several rural subdivisions (panchayats).[[98]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-Administrative_divisions_of_Jharkhand-99)

## Economy
The [gross domestic product](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gross_domestic_product "Gross domestic product") of Jharkhand is estimated at ₹5.56 lakh crore (US$66 billion) in 2025–26. The per capita GDP of Jharkhand in 2024-25 was ₹115,960 (US$1,400).[[100]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-101)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/60/Coal_Mine.jpg/250px-Coal_Mine.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Coal_Mine.jpg)Open-cast coal mining in [Dhanbad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dhanbad "Dhanbad")
Jharkhand has several towns and innumerable villages with civic amenities. Urbanization ratio is 24.1%.[[101]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-102) Jharkhand also has immense [mineral](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mineral "Mineral") resources: minerals ranging from (ranking in the country within bracket) from [iron](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iron "Iron") [ore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ore "Ore") (4th), [coal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coal "Coal") (3rd), [copper](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Copper "Copper") ore (1st), [mica](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mica "Mica") (1st), [bauxite](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bauxite "Bauxite") (3rd), [manganese](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manganese "Manganese"), [limestone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limestone "Limestone"), [china clay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/China_clay "China clay"), [fire clay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fire_clay "Fire clay"), [graphite](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Graphite "Graphite") (8th), [kainite](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kainite "Kainite") (1st), [chromite](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chromite "Chromite") (2nd), [asbestos](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asbestos "Asbestos") (1st), [thorium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thorium "Thorium") (3rd), [sillimanite](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sillimanite "Sillimanite"), [uranium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uranium "Uranium") (Jaduguda mines, Narwa Pahar) (1st) and even [gold](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gold "Gold") ([Rakha Mines](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rakha_Mines "Rakha Mines")) (6th) and [silver](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Silver "Silver") and several other minerals. Large deposits of coal and iron ore support concentration of [industry](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Industrial_sector "Industrial sector"), in centres like [Jamshedpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jamshedpur "Jamshedpur"), [Dhanbad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dhanbad "Dhanbad"), [Bokaro](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bokaro_Steel_City "Bokaro Steel City") and Ranchi. [Tata Steel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tata_Steel "Tata Steel"), a _[NSE NIFTY 500](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NSE_NIFTY_500 "NSE NIFTY 500")_ conglomerate has its corporate office and main plant in Tatanagar, Jharkhand.[[102]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-103) It reported a gross income of ₹. 204,910 million for 2005. NTPC will start coal production from its captive mine in state in 2011–12, for which the company will be investing about Rs 18 billion.[[103]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-104)
In February 2006, the government of Jharkhand established the Jharkhand Silk Textile and Handicraft Development Corporation (Jharcraft), which promotes local sericulture and weaving and the wider marketing of these products.[[104]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-105)[[105]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-106)
Agriculture is another major economic sector. Farmers in Jharkhand produce several crops such as rice, wheat, maize, pulses, potatoes, and vegetables such as tomato, carrots, cabbage, brinjal, pumpkin, and papaya. Other important industries include the cottage industry and IT.[[106]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-107)
## Culture
Main article: [Culture of Jharkhand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Culture_of_Jharkhand "Culture of Jharkhand")
### Cuisine
See also: [Cuisine of Jharkhand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cuisine_of_Jharkhand "Cuisine of Jharkhand")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3e/Rice_plate_Jharkhand.jpg/250px-Rice_plate_Jharkhand.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Rice_plate_Jharkhand.jpg)A Jharkhand Rice Plate
Staple foods of Jharkhand are [rice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice "Rice"), [dal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dal "Dal"), [roti](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roti "Roti"), [vegetables](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vegetable "Vegetable"), and [tubers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tuber "Tuber"). Spices are sparingly used in cuisine. Famous dishes include [Chhilka Roti](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chhilka_Roti "Chhilka Roti"), [Malpua](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Malpua "Malpua"), [Pitha](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pitha "Pitha"), [Dhooska](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dhooska "Dhooska"), Arsa roti, Dudhauri, and [Panipuri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panipuri "Panipuri") (Gupchup).[[107]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-108)[[108]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-109) Rugra and Putoo is a type of edible mushroom that is grown extensively in Jharkhand and harvested during the rainy months. It has a hardened, white, edible shell and a softer dark coloured centre. [Bamboo shoot](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bamboo_shoot "Bamboo shoot") are a versatile ingredient used in many culinary traditions, particularly in Jharkhand cuisine. They can be boiled, steamed, stir-fried, or pickled, making them a popular addition to dishes such as soups, curries, and salads. Bamboo shoots are known for their ability to absorb the flavours of the ingredients they are cooked with, enhancing the overall taste of the dishes.[[109]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-110) The leaves of Munga ([Moringa oleifera](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moringa_oleifera "Moringa oleifera")) and Koinar ([Bauhinia variegata](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bauhinia_variegata "Bauhinia variegata")) trees are used as a leafy vegetable or [Saag](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saag "Saag").[[110]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-111)
Local alcoholic drinks include rice beer, originally known as Handi or [Handia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Handia_\(drink\) "Handia \(drink\)"), named after the vessel, handi (earthen pot), used to make it. Handia is culturally associated with natives, i.e., Sadans and Tribals; this drink is consumed by both men and women on social occasions like marriage and festivals.[[111]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-112)[[112]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-113) Another common liquor is called _Mahua[daru](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Desi_daru "Desi daru")_, made from flowers of the Mahua tree ([Madhuca longifolia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Madhuca_longifolia "Madhuca longifolia")).[[113]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-114)
### Folk music and dance
See also: [Folk dances of Jharkhand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folk_dances_of_Jharkhand "Folk dances of Jharkhand")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/eb/Chhau_Dancers.jpg/250px-Chhau_Dancers.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Chhau_Dancers.jpg)Chhau Dancers in Jharkhand village
There are several folk dances in Jharkhand such as: [Jhumair](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jhumair "Jhumair"), [Mardani Jhumar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mardani_Jhumar "Mardani Jhumar"), [Janani Jhumar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Janani_Jhumar "Janani Jhumar"), [Domkach](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Domkach "Domkach"), Vinsariya, Jhumta, Fagua, [Angnai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Janani_Jhumar "Janani Jhumar"), [Paiki](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paiki_dance "Paiki dance"), [Chhau](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chhau_dance "Chhau dance"), [Firkal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Firkal_dance "Firkal dance"), [Mundari](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Munda_people#Culture_and_tradition "Munda people"), and [Santali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Santal_people#Culture "Santal people") dance.[[114]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-115)
### Festivals
Major festivals of Jharkhand are [Sarhul](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sarhul "Sarhul"), [Baha parab](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baha_parab "Baha parab"), [Karam Parab](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Karam_festival "Karam festival"), [Mage Parab](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mage_Parab "Mage Parab"), [Sohrai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sohrai "Sohrai"), [Bandna](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bandna "Bandna"), [Tusu Festival](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tusu_Festival "Tusu Festival"), [Makar Sankranti](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Makar_Sankranti "Makar Sankranti"), [Nawakhani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nawakhani "Nawakhani"), [Durga Puja](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Durga_Puja "Durga Puja"), [Jitiya](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jitiya "Jitiya"), [Manasa Puja](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manasa "Manasa"), [Rath Yatra](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rath_Yatra "Rath Yatra"), [Saraswati Puja](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saraswati_Puja "Saraswati Puja"), [Diwali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diwali "Diwali"), [Holi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holi "Holi"), Phagua and the Sendra festival.[[115]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-Khanna-2010-116)

### Paintings
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4e/A_Munda_tribesman_sitting_in_front_of_wall_decorated_with_Munda_style_Sohrai_Painting_at_Isko_Village%2C_Hazaribagh.jpg/250px-A_Munda_tribesman_sitting_in_front_of_wall_decorated_with_Munda_style_Sohrai_Painting_at_Isko_Village%2C_Hazaribagh.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:A_Munda_tribesman_sitting_in_front_of_wall_decorated_with_Munda_style_Sohrai_Painting_at_Isko_Village,_Hazaribagh.jpg)Sohrai wall painting at Isko Village, [Hazaribagh district](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hazaribagh_district "Hazaribagh district"), Jharkhand
[Sohrai and Khovar painting](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sohrai_and_Khovar_painting "Sohrai and Khovar painting") is a mural art form practised by women. Sohrai painting is traditionally done at the [Sohrai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sohrai "Sohrai") harvest festival, while Khovar painting is done at weddings.[[115]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-Khanna-2010-116)
### Tattoo
The tattoo making tradition of [Godna](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Godna "Godna") is an essential part of local tradition.[[115]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-Khanna-2010-116)
### Cinema
Main article: [Cinema of Jharkhand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cinema_of_Jharkhand "Cinema of Jharkhand")
Jharkhand produces many films in regional and Tribal languages including [Nagpuri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nagpuri_cinema "Nagpuri cinema"), [Khortha](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khortha_cinema "Khortha cinema"), [Santali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Santali_cinema "Santali cinema"), [Ho](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ho_language "Ho language"), and [Kurukh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kurukh_language "Kurukh language").[[116]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-117) The film industry in the state of Jharkhand is known as **Jhollywood**.[[117]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-118)[[118]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-119)
## Media
There are some television channels, newspapers, and radio stations which operate in Jharkhand. [DD Jharkhand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DD_Jharkhand "DD Jharkhand") is an important channel in Jharkhand. [All India Radio](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/All_India_Radio "All India Radio") operates from Ranchi.[[119]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-120)
[Hindustan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hindustan_\(newspaper\) "Hindustan \(newspaper\)"), [Dainik Jagran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dainik_Jagran "Dainik Jagran"), Sokal Sokal, [Prabhat Khabar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prabhat_Khabar "Prabhat Khabar"), and Ranchi Express are some of the Hindi newspapers and [The Hindu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Hindu "The Hindu"), [The Times of India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Times_of_India "The Times of India"), [Hindustan Times](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hindustan_Times "Hindustan Times"), [Navbharat Times](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Navbharat_Times "Navbharat Times"), [The Pioneer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Pioneer_\(India\) "The Pioneer \(India\)"), and [The Telegraph](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Telegraph_\(Calcutta\) "The Telegraph \(Calcutta\)") are some English newspapers in Jharkhand. 
## Transport
### Airways
  * [Birsa Munda Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Birsa_Munda_Airport "Birsa Munda Airport") is the largest domestic airport in the state with air connectivity to major Indian cities of [Delhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Delhi "Delhi"), [Kolkata](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kolkata "Kolkata"), [Bangalore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bangalore "Bangalore"), [Mumbai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai"), [Hyderabad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyderabad "Hyderabad") among others.[[120]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-121)
  * [Deoghar Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deoghar_Airport "Deoghar Airport") is the second busiest airport located in the state of Jharkhand, [India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/India "India"). It is the second operational airport in state of Jharkhand after [Ranchi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi "Ranchi").[[121]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-122)
  * [Sonari Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sonari_Airport "Sonari Airport") at [Jamshedpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jamshedpur "Jamshedpur") is the third operational airport in the state and it has daily flight to [Bhubaneswar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhubaneswar "Bhubaneswar") and [Kolkata](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kolkata "Kolkata").
  * [Bokaro Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bokaro_Airport "Bokaro Airport") is under [Steel Authority of India Limited](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Steel_Authority_of_India_Limited "Steel Authority of India Limited") and 4th operational airport of the state. It is being developed under [UDAN](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UDAN "UDAN") scheme.
  * Other airports present in the state are [Chakulia Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chakulia_Airport "Chakulia Airport"), [Dumka Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dumka_Airport "Dumka Airport"), [Giridih Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Giridih_Airport "Giridih Airport"), [Dhalbhumgarh Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dhalbhumgarh_Airport "Dhalbhumgarh Airport") and [Dhanbad Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dhanbad_Airport "Dhanbad Airport") which mostly run private and [charter flights](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Charter_flights "Charter flights").[[122]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-123)

### Ports
Jharkhand is landlocked state but has numerous rivers and waterways.[[126]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-127) A [multi-modal port](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sahebganj_Multi-Modal_Port "Sahebganj Multi-Modal Port") has been planned at [Sahebganj](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sahebganj "Sahebganj") where river [Ganges](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ganges "Ganges") flows.[[127]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-128) The project is estimated to cost ₹65,000 million and phase-1 is estimated to be completed by 2019.[[128]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-129)
### Railways
See also: [Railway stations in Jharkhand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Railway_stations_in_Jharkhand "Railway stations in Jharkhand")
Jharkhand is very well connected by railways. The state has numerous [railway stations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Railway_stations_in_Jharkhand "Railway stations in Jharkhand") and railway junctions.[[129]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-130) [Dhanbad Junction](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dhanbad_Junction "Dhanbad Junction") is the largest railway station in the state which is connected to almost all big cities of India. Hilly regions of state are equipped with tunnels that form essential organ of railways.

## Education
As per the 2011 census conducted by [Government of India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Government_of_India "Government of India") the official literacy rate for the state was 66.41% (male: 76.84%; female: 55.42%) with nine districts above the average literacy rate:[[130]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-131)[[131]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-132)
  * [Ranchi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district "Ranchi district"): 76.06% (male: 84.26%; female: 67.44%)
  * [East Singhbhum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Singhbhum_district "East Singhbhum district"): 75.49% (male: 83.75%; female: 66.81%)
  * [Dhanbad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dhanbad_district "Dhanbad district"): 74.52% (male: 83.81%; Female: 64.29%)
  * [Ramgarh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ramgarh_district "Ramgarh district"): 73.17% (male: 82.44%; female: 63.09%)
  * [Bokaro](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bokaro_district "Bokaro district"): 72.01% (male: 82.51%; female: 60.63%)
  * [Hazaribagh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hazaribagh_district "Hazaribagh district"): 69.75% (male: 80.01%; female: 58.95%)
  * [Simdega](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Simdega_district "Simdega district"): 67.99% (male: 76.08%; female: 59.92%)
  * [Saraikela Khasawan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Seraikela_Kharsawan_district "Seraikela Kharsawan district"): 67.70% (male: 79.03%; female: 55.88%)
  * [Lohardaga](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lohardaga_district "Lohardaga district"): 67.61% (male: 77.21%; female: 57.69%)
  * [Koderma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Koderma_district "Koderma district"): 66.84% (male: 79.78%; female: 53.23%)


Since the formation of the new state, the Jharkhand Education Project Council (JEPC) has been implementing four projects to spread elementary education: DPEP, SSA, NPEGEL, and KGBV. The state has been moving towards the goal of universal elementary education but the target of 100% enrolment and retention of children in schools has not yet been attained.[[132]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-133) Jharkhand has made primary education so accessible that 95% of children of ages 6–11 are enrolled in school, as opposed to 56% in 1993–94; this will likely improve literacy a great deal.[_[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")_]
### Schools
The medium of instruction in schools is [Hindi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hindi "Hindi")/[English](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/English_language "English language") with [English](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/English_language "English language")/[Hindi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hindi "Hindi")/[Sanskrit](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sanskrit "Sanskrit")/[Bengali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bengali_language "Bengali language")/[Odia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Odia_language "Odia language") as second language. After 10 years of schooling, students can join two years of Intermediate course (or +2 courses) in [Arts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arts "Arts"), [Science](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Science "Science") and [Commerce](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Commerce "Commerce"). This is followed by three years of degree courses (graduation) or four years of [Engineering](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Engineering "Engineering")/[Agriculture](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Agriculture "Agriculture")/[Medicine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Medicine "Medicine") degree. 
The school system comprises various private and public schools. The government schools are abundant. Few notable schools are: [St. Thomas School, Ranchi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._Thomas_School,_Ranchi "St. Thomas School, Ranchi"), [Sainik School Tilaiya](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sainik_School,_Tilaiya "Sainik School, Tilaiya"), [Loyola School, Jamshedpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Loyola_School,_Jamshedpur "Loyola School, Jamshedpur"), [Delhi Public School, Bokaro](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Delhi_Public_School,_Bokaro "Delhi Public School, Bokaro"), [Delhi Public School, Ranchi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Delhi_Public_School,_Ranchi "Delhi Public School, Ranchi"), [Bishop Westcott Boys' School](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bishop_Westcott_Boys%27_School "Bishop Westcott Boys' School"), [Ramakrishna Mission Vidyapith, Deoghar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ramakrishna_Mission_Vidyapith,_Deoghar "Ramakrishna Mission Vidyapith, Deoghar"), [De Nobili School](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/De_Nobili_School,_FRI "De Nobili School, FRI") and [St. Xavier's School, Hazaribagh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._Xavier%27s_School,_Hazaribagh "St. Xavier's School, Hazaribagh"). 
In 2009 [Franz Gastler](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Franz_Gastler "Franz Gastler") established [Yuwa School](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yuwa-India "Yuwa-India") a NGO in [Hutup](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hutup "Hutup") village in Ranchi district with helps of friends to use football as a platform to combat [child marriage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Child_marriage_in_India "Child marriage in India"), [illiteracy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Education_in_India#Women's_education "Education in India") and [human trafficking](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Human_trafficking_in_India "Human trafficking in India") in rural India.[[133]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-134) In 2019, It won the Laureus Sport for Good Award.[[134]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-135)

## Public health
Because of its mild climate, Jharkhand, particularly its capital Ranchi, has been a health resort. As far back as 1918, facilities were set up for treatment of [mentally challenged](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mentally_challenged "Mentally challenged").[[135]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-136)
European Mental Hospital was established along with Indian Mental Hospital. Today they are called Central Institute of Psychiatry and Ranchi Institute of Neuro-psychiatry and Allied Sciences respectively. In certain areas of Jharkhand, poverty and consequent malnutrition have given rise to diseases like [tuberculosis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tuberculosis "Tuberculosis") (TB). In fact, TB has assumed [epidemic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epidemic "Epidemic") proportions in certain areas of the state. For management and treatment of such TB, Itki TB Sanatorium, Ranchi, established in 1928 has been doing work as a premier institute for clinical and programmatic management of TB. The Itki TB Sanatorium is well equipped and accredited by the Indian government for quality assurance and Culture and Drug Sensitivity Testing for M.TB. It provides free of cost treatment for TB as well as drug-resistant TB. Likewise, in the field of treatment of cancer, Tata Main Hospital, Jamshedpur,[[136]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-137) is rendering pioneering work. In the same way, Bokaro General Hospital equipped with modern facilities for the treatment of cancer and heart-related problems with the capacity of 1100 beds one of the largest in eastern India. 
Although several public and private health facilities are available in the state, overall infrastructure for dispensing health related services require improvements. An exception is the Tata Motors Hospital which is an example of an ISO 14001 and 18001 certified hospital with DNB teaching facilities.[_[citation needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")_]
Ranchi, the capital, has witnessed a sharp growth in the number of hospitals. 
[Fluoride](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fluoride "Fluoride") in groundwater presents a public health problem in Jharkhand. A recent survey led by the [Birla Institute of Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Birla_Institute_of_Technology "Birla Institute of Technology"), Mesra, Ranchi in collaboration with [UNICEF](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UNICEF "UNICEF") in the northwest districts of [Palamau](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palamau "Palamau") and [Garhwa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Garhwa "Garhwa") found fluoride levels above the drinking [WHO](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/WHO "WHO") drinking water guidelines.[[137]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-138) Excessive amounts of fluoride in drinking water can lead to [dental fluorosis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dental_fluorosis "Dental fluorosis"), prevalent bone fractures, and [skeletal fluorosis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Skeletal_fluorosis "Skeletal fluorosis"), an irreversible disabling condition.[[138]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-139) Some work has focused on combating fluorosis through increased calcium intake by consuming local plants.[[139]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-140) Researchers at [Princeton University](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Princeton_University "Princeton University") and the Birla Institute of Technology, Mesra, Ranchi are currently investigating defluoridation options, while performing an epidemiological survey to assess the extent of fluoride linked health problems and the impact of future interventions.[[140]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-141)[[141]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-142)
Almost 80% of Jharkhand's people are farmers, although it contains 40% of India's mineral reserves it has some of India's poorest people, in Summer 2009 the state was threatened by drought, with people criticising the government for not providing food aid or assistance.[[142]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-143)
## Sports
[JRD TATA Sports Complex, Jamshedpur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/JRD_Tata_Sports_Complex "JRD Tata Sports Complex") hosts football matches of [Indian Super League](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Super_League "Indian Super League") and is the home of ISL football club [Jamshedpur FC](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jamshedpur_FC "Jamshedpur FC"). [Cricket](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cricket "Cricket"), [hockey](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey"), and [football](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Association_football "Association football") are common sports in Jharkhand. [Jaipal Singh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaipal_Singh "Jaipal Singh"), a former Indian hockey captain and [Olympian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Olympic_Games "Olympic Games"), and [Manohar Topno](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manohar_Topno "Manohar Topno") former [Indian hockey team](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/India_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "India men's national field hockey team") player were from Jharkhand. Singh was the captain of the hockey team that won the first gold medal for India in the [1928 Summer Olympics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1928_Summer_Olympics "1928 Summer Olympics") in [Amsterdam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amsterdam "Amsterdam"). [Mahendra Singh Dhoni](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mahendra_Singh_Dhoni "Mahendra Singh Dhoni"), a former captain of [Indian cricket team](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cricket_team "Indian cricket team") who led the [Indian cricket team](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/India_national_cricket_team "India national cricket team") to [ICC Cricket World Cup](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ICC_Cricket_World_Cup "ICC Cricket World Cup") glory on 2 April 2011, 28 years after former Indian captain [Kapil Dev](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kapil_Dev "Kapil Dev") won the World Cup in 1983 at [Lord's, England](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lord%27s "Lord's"), is from here.[[143]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-144)
Other notable cricketers from Jharkhand are [Varun Aaron](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Varun_Aaron "Varun Aaron"), [Shahbaz Nadeem](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shahbaz_Nadeem "Shahbaz Nadeem"), and [Saurabh Tiwary](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saurabh_Tiwary "Saurabh Tiwary"). He was one of the key batsmen in the Indian team that won the [2008 U/19 Cricket World Cup](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2008_Under-19_Cricket_World_Cup "2008 Under-19 Cricket World Cup") in [Malaysia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia"). Other sportspeople include [Deepika Kumari](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deepika_Kumari "Deepika Kumari"), an archer who won gold medal in the 2010 Commonwealth games in the women's individual recurve event. [Nikki Pradhan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nikki_Pradhan "Nikki Pradhan"), a member of the [national hockey team](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/India_women%27s_national_field_hockey_team "India women's national field hockey team"), is the first female hockey player from Jharkhand to represent India at the [Olympics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Olympic_Games "Olympic Games"). 
An [International Cricket stadium with an indoor stadium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/JSCA_International_Cricket_Stadium "JSCA International Cricket Stadium") and a practice ground was constructed. It hosted a One-Day International match between India and England on 19 January 2013.[[144]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-145) The stadium has hosted two [IPL 6](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IPL_6 "IPL 6") matches for [KKR](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kolkata_Knight_Riders "Kolkata Knight Riders") and qualifier 2 of [IPL 8](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IPL_8 "IPL 8") between [CSK](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chennai_Super_Kings "Chennai Super Kings") and [RCB](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Royal_Challengers_Bangalore "Royal Challengers Bangalore") in addition to Celebrity Cricket League Matches for Bhojpuri Dabanggs. A tennis academy, which was inaugurated by [Sania Mirza](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sania_Mirza "Sania Mirza") and [Shoaib Malik](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shoaib_Malik "Shoaib Malik"), also runs besides the cricket stadium.[[145]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-146) Ranchi is among six cities in [Hockey India League](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hockey_India_League "Hockey India League") to be played in January 2013. Ranchi franchise was bought by Patel-Uniexcel Group and the team named [Ranchi Rhinos](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_Rhinos "Ranchi Rhinos"). It is now being co-hosted by Mahendra Singh Dhoni and was named as [Ranchi Rays](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_Rays "Ranchi Rays").[[146]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-147)

## Tourism
Main article: [Tourism in Jharkhand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tourism_in_Jharkhand "Tourism in Jharkhand")
Jharkhand is known for its waterfalls, hills and holy places.[[147]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-148)[[16]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-nyoooz.com-16) [Parasnath](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parasnath "Parasnath"), [Baidyanath Dham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baidyanath_Dham "Baidyanath Dham"), [Maa Dewri Temple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maa_Dewri_Temple "Maa Dewri Temple") and [Chhinnamasta Temple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chhinnamasta_Temple "Chhinnamasta Temple") are major religious places.[[148]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-149)[[149]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-150)
Tattapani Hot Water Spring is located 8 km from [Latehar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Latehar "Latehar"). The hot spring water come out from different places on the Sukari River bed. Rich in sulfur, the hot spring is believed to have medicinal properties and good for skin.[[150]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-151)
[Itkhori](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Itkhori_Block "Itkhori Block") is a holy place for Hindus, Buddhists and Jains. It is believed to be the place from where [Gautama Buddha](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gautama_Buddha "Gautama Buddha") started his journey for [Bodh Gaya](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bodh_Gaya "Bodh Gaya"). Many sculptures of Hindu, Jain and Buddhist art styles were found in 2018.[[151]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-152)[[152]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-153) [Rankini Temple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rankini_Temple,_Jadugora "Rankini Temple, Jadugora") of [Jadugora](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jadugora "Jadugora") is famous in Jharkhand, as well as in Odisha, West Bengal and Bihar.[[153]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-154)[[154]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-155) There are several waterfalls in the state including [Jonha Falls](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jonha_Falls "Jonha Falls"), [Hundru Falls](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hundru_Falls "Hundru Falls"), [Dassam Falls](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dassam_Falls "Dassam Falls"), [Perwaghagh Falls](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perwaghagh_Falls "Perwaghagh Falls") and [Panchghagh Falls](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panchghagh_Falls "Panchghagh Falls").[[155]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-156)[[156]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-157)[[157]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-158) [Netarhat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Netarhat "Netarhat") is a hill station in the state.[[158]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-159)[[159]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-160)[[160]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-161)
There are several wildlife sanctuaries and national park in Jharkhand including [Betla National Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betla_National_Park "Betla National Park") and [Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dalma_Wildlife_Sanctuary "Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary"), which are major attraction for tourists.[[161]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-162)[[162]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-163)[[163]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-164)[[164]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-165)
[State Museum Hotwar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/State_Museum_Hotwar "State Museum Hotwar") and [Tribal Research Institute and Museum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tribal_Research_Institute_and_Museum "Tribal Research Institute and Museum") showcase various cultural heritage and tribal culture of Jharkhand.[[165]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-166)[[166]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand#cite_note-167)

**Ranchi district** is the most populous district of [Jharkhand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jharkhand "Jharkhand") [state](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/States_and_territories_of_India "States and territories of India") in eastern [India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/India "India"). [Ranchi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi "Ranchi"),[[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-:0-2) the capital of Jharkhand, is the district headquarters. 
## History
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Ranchi_district&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]
Main article: [History of Ranchi district](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_Ranchi_district "History of Ranchi district")
Iron slag, potsherds and iron tools have been found in the Chota Nagpur plateau dated to 1400 BCE.[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-3)
[Magadha Empire](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Magadha_\(Mahajanapada\) "Magadha \(Mahajanapada\)") exercised indirect control over the territory, which lasted until the reign of the [Ashoka](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ashoka "Ashoka"). [Kalinga](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kalinga_\(historical_kingdom\) "Kalinga \(historical kingdom\)") rulers are believed to have ravaged the region during their expeditions to [Rajgir](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rajgir "Rajgir") and [Pataliputra](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pataliputra "Pataliputra"). Armies of [Samudra Gupta](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Samudragupta "Samudragupta") passed through the region on their expedition to [Deccan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deccan_Plateau "Deccan Plateau"). During medieval period, the [Nagvanshi dynasty](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nagvanshis_of_Chotanagpur "Nagvanshis of Chotanagpur") were ruling in the region. [Khukhragarh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khukhragarh "Khukhragarh") was one of the capitals of Nagvanshi dynasty.[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-4) With the expansion of the [Mughal Empire](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mughal_Empire "Mughal Empire"), the Nagvanshis were forced to pay tribute, but they continued to rule and administer independently until the advent of the [East India Company](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/British_East_India_Company "British East India Company"). [Thakur Vishwanath Shahdeo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vishwanath_Shahdeo "Vishwanath Shahdeo"), [Pandey Ganpat Rai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pandey_Ganpat_Rai "Pandey Ganpat Rai"), [Tikait Umrao Singh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tikait_Umrao_Singh "Tikait Umrao Singh") and [Sheikh Bhikhari](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sheikh_Bhikhari "Sheikh Bhikhari") played pivotal role in [Indian Rebellion of 1857](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Rebellion_of_1857 "Indian Rebellion of 1857").[[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-Ustad1997-5)[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-6) During the period of the [British Raj](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/British_Raj "British Raj"), [Adivasis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Adivasi "Adivasi") and other locals of the Chotanagpur Plateau continued to oppose the subjugation by the [British](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/British_Empire "British Empire"), and the region witnessed a number of uprisings and revolts.

Ranchi[[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-:0-2) has a [humid subtropical climate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Humid_subtropical_climate "Humid subtropical climate"). However, due to its position and the forests around the city, it is known for its pleasant climate. Its climate is the primary reason why Ranchi was once the summer capital of the undivided State of Bihar. Ranchi used to be a preferable hill station in the past. Temperature ranges from maximum 42 to 20 °C during summer, and from 25 to 0 °C during winter. December and January are the coolest months with temperature getting to freezing point in some places of the city. The annual rainfall is about 1430 mm (56.34 inches). From June to September the rainfall is about 1,100 mm. Ranchi is located at 23°21′N 85°20′E.[5] The total area covered by the Ranchi municipal area is 175.12 square kilometres and the average elevation of the city is 651 m above sea level. 
Ranchi is located on the southern part of the Chota Nagpur plateau which forms the eastern edge of the Deccan plateau. Ranchi is referred to as the "City of Waterfalls", due to the presence of numerous large and small falls of around the close vicinity of the city. The most popular ones are [Dassam Falls](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dassam_Falls "Dassam Falls"),[[7]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-7) [Hundru Falls](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hundru_Falls "Hundru Falls"), [Jonha Falls](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jonha_Falls "Jonha Falls"), and [Panchghagh Falls](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panchghagh_Falls "Panchghagh Falls"). 
The [Subarnarekha river](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Subarnarekha_River "Subarnarekha River") and its tributaries constitute the local river system. Dams in Kanke, Rukka and Hatia have been built over these channels to cater to the water requirements of majority the population. 
Ranchi has a hilly topography and its combination with dense tropical forests ensures that it enjoys a comparatively moderate climate compared to the rest of the state. Previously, it was accorded a 'hill station' status during the British rule. Rapid population growth and industrialization have caused considerable change in the weather pattern and rise in average temperatures. This has resulted in gradual loss of this "Hill Station" like status. It also has numerous dams constructed in different regions of the city inside and on the outskirts. 
## Divisions
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Ranchi_district&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Divisions")]
Presently, Ranchi district is divided into 2 sub-divisions and 14 administrative blocks. On 12 September 2007, [Khunti district](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khunti_district "Khunti district") was created by carving Khunti subdivision and its 6 blocks out of Ranchi district. Ranchi sub-division is further divided into 11 blocks: [Angara](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Angara_block "Angara block"), [Burmu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Burmu_block "Burmu block"), [Bero](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bero_block "Bero block"), [Chanho](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chanho_block "Chanho block"), [Kanke](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kanke_block "Kanke block"), [Lapung](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lapung_block "Lapung block"), [Mandar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mandar_block "Mandar block"), [Namkum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Namkum_block "Namkum block"), [Ormanjhi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ormanjhi_block "Ormanjhi block"), [Ratu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ratu_block "Ratu block") and [Silli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Silli_block "Silli block"). Bundu sub-division comprises 3 blocks: [Bundu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bundu_block "Bundu block"), [Sonahatu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sonahatu_block "Sonahatu block"), and [Tamar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tamar_block "Tamar block"), The district has 7 [Assembly constituencies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_constituencies_of_the_Jharkhand_Legislative_Assembly "List of constituencies of the Jharkhand Legislative Assembly"), namely, [Tamar (ST)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tamar_Assembly_constituency "Tamar Assembly constituency"), [Silli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Silli_Assembly_constituency "Silli Assembly constituency"), [Khijri (ST)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khijri_Assembly_constituency "Khijri Assembly constituency"), [Ranchi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_Assembly_constituency "Ranchi Assembly constituency"), [Hatia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hatia_Assembly_constituency "Hatia Assembly constituency"), [Kanke (SC)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kanke_Assembly_constituency "Kanke Assembly constituency"), and [Mandar (ST)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mandar_Assembly_constituency "Mandar Assembly constituency"). Silli, Khijri, Ranchi, Hatia and Kanke are part of [Ranchi Lok Sabha constituency](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_Lok_Sabha_constituency "Ranchi Lok Sabha constituency").

## Education
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Ranchi_district&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Education")]
Ranchi district[[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-:0-2) boasts of many premier institutes in the field of higher education. This may be a reason that Ranchi has an average literacy rate of 77.13% (census 2011), higher than the national average of 74.04%: male literacy is 85.63%, and female literacy is 68.2%. Notable schools in Ranchi include [Delhi Public School, Ranchi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Delhi_Public_School,_Ranchi "Delhi Public School, Ranchi"), [Kairali School](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kairali_School "Kairali School") and [St. Thomas School, Ranchi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._Thomas_School,_Ranchi "St. Thomas School, Ranchi"). 
  * [Amity University, Jharkhand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amity_University,_Jharkhand "Amity University, Jharkhand") also known as Amity University, Ranchi, is a private university.


  * [Birsa Agricultural University](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Birsa_Agricultural_University "Birsa Agricultural University") at Ranchi was established in 1981.


  * [Birla Institute of Technology, Mesra (BIT, Mesra)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Birla_Institute_of_Technology,_Mesra "Birla Institute of Technology, Mesra") at Mesra, Ranchi was established in 1955.


  * [Central Institute of Psychiatry](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_Institute_of_Psychiatry "Central Institute of Psychiatry") (CIP Ranchi), offers higher level study in medical education. It is jointly administered by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare and Directorate General of Health Services. It is also a psychiatry unit accepting patients from all over India and also Bhutan and Nepal. The British established this hospital in 1918 as the Ranchi European Lunatic Asylum.


  * [Central University of Jharkhand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_University_of_Jharkhand "Central University of Jharkhand") at Ranchi was established by an act of Indian Parliament (Act no. 25 of 2009) in 2009. Like other Central Universities of India it is a teaching and research university. The university is functional in its temporary 45-acre campus located at Brambe, Mandar.


  * [IIIT Ranchi or IIITR (Indian Institute of Information Technology, Ranchi)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Information_Technology,_Ranchi "Indian Institute of Information Technology, Ranchi") Established in 2016, it mainly offers undergraduate programmes for computer science and electronics.


  * [Indian Institute of Management Ranchi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Management_Ranchi "Indian Institute of Management Ranchi") (IIM Ranchi), the eighth Indian Institute of Management was established at Ranchi in 2010. It currently offers a two-year PGDM as its flagship program and has launched PFPEX recently for research work.


  * [National Institute of Advanced Manufacturing Technology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Institute_of_Advanced_Manufacturing_Technology "National Institute of Advanced Manufacturing Technology") (NIAMT) formerly known as the National Institute of Foundry and Forge Technology (NIFFT) was set up in 1966 by the Government of India in collaboration with UNDP-UNESCO to provide quality engineers and well trained specialists for running Manufacturing, Metallurgical, Foundry and Forge industries.


  * [National University of Study and Research in Law](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_University_of_Study_and_Research_in_Law "National University of Study and Research in Law"), Ranchi was established by a legislative act of the Government of Jharkhand as the fourteenth national law university of India. It offers graduate and post-graduate courses in law.


  * [Rajendra Institute of Medical Sciences](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rajendra_Institute_of_Medical_Sciences "Rajendra Institute of Medical Sciences") a medical institute of Ranchi University in Ranchi, was established in 2002 by upgrading the then Rajendra Medical College Hospital (RMCH) which was established in 1960. The courses offered are MBBS, M.D., Mch Neurosurgery, BDS, BSc Nursing, BSc(Post Basic) Nursing, GNM, Paramedical and Physiotherapy.


  * [Ranchi University](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_University "Ranchi University"), presently comprising 35 constituent colleges and 29 affiliated colleges, was established in 1960. One of its constituent college, [St. Xavier's College](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._Xavier%27s_College,_Ranchi "St. Xavier's College, Ranchi") at Ranchi was established in 1944.


  * [Xavier Institute of Social Service](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xavier_Institute_of_Social_Service "Xavier Institute of Social Service")(XISS), Ranchi started in 1955 to educate young graduates in social work and management programs as an extension of St Xavier's college Ranchi. In 1975, the department registered itself as a separate institute and established their new campus in Purulia Road, Ranchi. The institute offers graduate and post-graduate course in management studies.

According to the [2011 census](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2011_census_of_India "2011 census of India") Ranchi district has a [population](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Demographics_of_India "Demographics of India") of 2,914,253,[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-districtcensus-1) roughly equal to the nation of [Jamaica](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jamaica "Jamaica")[[10]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-cia-10) or the US state of [Arkansas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arkansas "Arkansas").[[11]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-11) This gives it a ranking of 130th in India (out of a total of [640](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Districts_of_India "Districts of India")).[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-districtcensus-1) The district has a population density of 572 inhabitants per square kilometre (1,480/sq mi).[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-districtcensus-1) Its [population growth rate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Family_planning_in_India "Family planning in India") over the decade 2001-2011 was 23.9%.[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-districtcensus-1) Ranchi has a [sex ratio](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sex_ratio "Sex ratio") of 950 [females](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Women_in_India "Women in India") for every 1000 males,[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-districtcensus-1) and a [literacy rate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Literacy_in_India "Literacy in India") of 76.06%. 43.14% of the population lives in urban areas. [Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scheduled_Castes_and_Scheduled_Tribes "Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes") collectively account for 41.01% (SC 5.25% and ST 35.76%)[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-districtcensus-1) of the district's total population. The prominent scheduled communities are [Oraon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kurukh_people "Kurukh people") (18.2%), [Munda](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Munda_people "Munda people") (10.3%), [Lohra](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lohra_\(tribe\) "Lohra \(tribe\)") (2.46%), [Bedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bedia_\(caste\) "Bedia \(caste\)") (1.32%), [Ghasi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ghasi "Ghasi") (1.21%), [Mahli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mahli "Mahli") (1.09%), [Dhobi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dhobi "Dhobi") (0.73%), [Bhogta](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhogta "Bhogta") (0.7%), [Chamar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chamar "Chamar")/Mochi (0.63%), [Dusadh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dusadh "Dusadh") (0.36%), Pan (0.33%), [Karmali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Karmali_tribe "Karmali tribe") (0.32%), [Chik Baraik](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chik_Baraik "Chik Baraik") (0.32%), Dom (0.29%), [Kharia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kharia_people "Kharia people") (0.27%), [Bhuiya](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhuiya "Bhuiya") (0.2%), [Santal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Santal_people "Santal people") (0.19%), Turi (0.12%), [Rajwar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rajuar "Rajuar") (0.11%), Gorait (0.1%), Hari/Mehtar/Bhangi (0.08%), [Ho](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ho_people "Ho people") (0.07%), [Binjhia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Binjhia_Tribe "Binjhia Tribe") (0.07%), Pasi (0.07%), and [Kharwar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kharwar "Kharwar") (0.05%). Other communities such as Gond, Birhor, Bauri, Kol, [Kora](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kora_\(tribe\) "Kora \(tribe\)"), Chero, [Bhumij](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhumij_people "Bhumij people"), Kisan, Nat, [Mal Paharia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mal_Paharia_people "Mal Paharia people"), [Musahar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Musahar "Musahar"), [Banjara](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Banjara_\(tribe\) "Banjara \(tribe\)"), and Asur/Agaria also have populations ranging between one thousand to a hundred.[[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-12)

At the time of the [2011 Census of India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2011_Census_of_India "2011 Census of India"), 30.23% of the population in the district spoke [Sadri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sadri_language "Sadri language"), 28.08% [Hindi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hindi "Hindi"), 8.55% [Urdu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Urdu "Urdu"), 7.52% [Kurukh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kurukh_language "Kurukh language"), 11.88% [Panchpargania](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panchpargania "Panchpargania"), 4.70% [Mundari](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mundari_language "Mundari language"), 2.51% [Bengali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bengali_language "Bengali language"), 2.17% [Bhojpuri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhojpuri_language "Bhojpuri language") and 1.17% [Magahi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Magahi_language "Magahi language") as their first language.[[13]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-language-13)

## Economy
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Ranchi_district&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Economy")]
In 2006 the Indian government named Ranchi one of the country's 250 [most backward districts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Poverty_in_India "Poverty in India") (out of a total of [640](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Districts_of_India "Districts of India")).[[16]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-brgf-16) It is one of the 23 districts in Jharkhand currently receiving funds from the Backward Regions Grant Fund Programme (BRGF).[[16]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-brgf-16) In the aspirational districts, Ranchi have improved.[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranchi_district#cite_note-17)

# TS TET 2024 admit cards expected soon; here’s how to download
## Eligible candidates can download their hall tickets from the official website tstet.cgg.gov.in.

![TS TET 2024 admit cards expected soon; here’s how to download](https://sc0.blr1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/article/103190-xxmvgstmya-1539677211.jpg)
The Department of School Education, Telangana will soon release the admit cards for the Telangana State Teachers Eligibility Test 2024 (TS TET 2024). Eligible candidates can download their hall tickets from the official website [tstet2024.aptonline.in/tstet/](https://tstet2024.aptonline.in/tstet/).
**The computer based test will be conducted between May 20 and June 3 in two shifts — 9.00 AM to 11.30 AM and 2.00 PM to 4.30 PM.** The validity period of TET qualifying certificates for appointment, unless otherwise notified by the Government of Telangana, would remain valid for life. Candidates can check more details available in the official notification.
### Steps to download TS TET 2024 admit card
  1. Visit the official website [tstet2024.aptonline.in/tstet/](https://tstet2024.aptonline.in/tstet/)
  2. On the homepage, click on TS TET 2024 admit card link
  3. Key in your login details and submit
  4. Check and download the admit card
  5. Take a printout for future reference


_For more details, candidates are advised to visit the official website_[ _here_](https://tstet2024.aptonline.in/tstet/) _._
